chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَجْمَعِ الْقُرْآنَ كُلَّهُ إِلَّا الْأَئِمَّةُ ( عليهم السلام ) وَ أَنَّهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَهُ كُلَّهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – No one collected the Quran, all of it, except for the Imams<sup>asws</sup>, and they<sup>asws</sup> are knowing its Knowledge, all of it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/32)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/32)
text: هُ قَالَ مَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَحَدٌ أَنْ يَدَّعِيَ أَنَّ عِنْدَهُ جَمِيعَ الْقُرْآنِ كُلِّهِ ظَاهِرِهِ وَ بَاطِنِهِ غَيْرُ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ammar Bin Marwan, from Al Mankhal, from Jabir,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘No one is able to claim that with him is the entirety of the Quran, all of it, it’s apparent, and its hidden, apart from the succesors<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn al-Husayn has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Muhammad ibn Sinan from ‘Ammar ibn Marwan from al-Munakhkhal from Jabir from abu Ja‘far (a.s.) who has said the following. “No one is able to claim that with him is the whole of the Holy Quran; its apparent and hidden essence, except the executors of the will of the Holy Prophet (s.a.).
